payload.answer: You want to recall open-ended ideas about knowledge graphs while planning to learn Python basics. Consider these open ideas: (1) foundational concepts (triples, RDF, URIs), (2) data modeling with ontologies and schemas, (3) querying with SPARQL, (4) graph embeddings for similarity and link prediction, (5) practical datasets and benchmarks (Wikidata, DBpedia), (6) common use cases (semantic search, data integration, provenance), (7) tooling and stacks (RDF stores, graph databases, Python libraries), (8) evaluation metrics for knowledge graphs, (9) pitfalls (missing data, schema drift, schema alignment), (10) learning path milestones bridging to Python basics.
